# omada_acl (Resource)

Manages an Omada gateway (OSG) ACL rule — the core building block of inter-VLAN segmentation on the Omada gateway. Targets the Open API v1 gateway ACL surface implemented by controller firmware such as 5.15.x. The rule `index` (order) is read-only: the controller owns ACL ordering via a site-global ModifyAclIndex call that this resource does not steer. Requires one of: `Site Settings Manager Modify`, `Network Config Page Modify`, or `Site Device Manager Modify`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Site ID that owns the ACL. Provide it via a variable or data source so the
# value is not hard-coded in checked-in configurations.
variable "site_id" {
  type = string
}

# A permissive, reversible probe rule: allow TCP from the IoT LAN network to the
# IoT LAN network (LAN-to-LAN). Use a dedicated, non-colliding source/destination
# and disable the rule (status = false) until the change is reviewed. ACLs are
# the core of inter-VLAN segmentation and can sever connectivity if misapplied.
resource "omada_acl" "probe" {
  site_id     = var.site_id
  description = "TF Probe ACL"
  status      = false # disabled until reviewed
  syslog      = false

  source_type      = 0 # network
  source_ids       = ["<iot-lan-network-id>"]
  destination_type = 0 # network
  destination_ids  = ["<iot-lan-network-id>"]
  policy           = 1   # allow
  protocols        = [6] # TCP
  state_mode       = 0   # auto

  direction = {
    lan_to_lan = true
  }
}

output "probe_acl_id" {
  value = omada_acl.probe.acl_id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `description` (String) ACL rule description (also the fallback identity used to locate the rule after create when the controller omits the id). Must contain 1 to 512 characters.
- `destination_type` (Number) Destination type. `0` = network; `1` = IP Group; `2` = IP-Port Group; `6` = IPv6 Group; `7` = IPv6-Port Group; `10` = Domain Group; `11` = !Network; `12` = !IP Group; `13` = !IP-Port Group; `14` = !IPv6 Group; `15` = !IPv6-Port Group.
- `direction` (Attributes) Gateway direction selector. At least one direction flag must be set; lan_to_lan conflicts with the other directions. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--direction))
- `policy` (Number) Rule action. `0` = drop; `1` = allow.
- `protocols` (List of Number) Protocol numbers; refer to section 5.5 of the Omada Open API Access Guide.
- `site_id` (String) Site ID to create the ACL in. Changing this forces replacement.
- `source_ids` (List of String) Source IDs, whose meaning depends on source_type (e.g. LAN network IDs when source_type is network).
- `source_type` (Number) Source type. `0` = network; `1` = IP Group; `2` = IP-Port Group; `4` = SSID; `6` = IPv6 Group; `7` = IPv6-Port Group; `8` = Country; `9` = Country Group; `11` = !Network; `12` = !IP Group; `13` = !IP-Port Group; `14` = !IPv6 Group; `15` = !IPv6-Port Group.
- `state_mode` (Number) Conntrack state match mode. `0` = auto; `1` = manual (use the states block).
- `status` (Boolean) Whether the rule is enabled.
- `syslog` (Boolean) Whether to log matches to remote syslog.

### Optional

- `destination_ids` (List of String) Destination IDs, whose meaning depends on destination_type. Optional on write (computed on read): the controller populates this for types that resolve ids.
- `states` (Attributes) Conntrack states to match. Only meaningful when state_mode is manual (1). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--states))
- `time_range_id` (String) Gateway ACL time range ID. Omit for an always-active rule.

### Read-Only

- `acl_id` (String) ACL ID assigned by the controller. Use it (with site_id) as the import target.
- `index` (Number) Rule order assigned by the controller (read-only). Omada reorders ACLs via a site-global, per-device-type call carrying the full ordered id map, so a single ACL resource cannot own its absolute index without fighting out-of-band changes. The resource observes the controller-assigned index and never sends it.

<a id="nestedatt--direction"></a>
### Nested Schema for `direction`

Optional:

- `lan_to_lan` (Boolean) Match LAN-to-LAN direction. Conflicts with the other directions.
- `lan_to_wan` (Boolean) Match LAN-to-WAN direction.
- `vpn_in_ids` (List of String) Selected VPN IDs (inbound) when matching VPN-sourced traffic.
- `wan_in_ids` (List of String) Selected WAN port IDs (inbound) when matching WAN-sourced traffic.


<a id="nestedatt--states"></a>
### Nested Schema for `states`

Optional:

- `established` (Boolean) Match the established state.
- `invalid` (Boolean) Match the invalid state.
- `related` (Boolean) Match the related state.
- `state_new` (Boolean) Match the new state.
